Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Simple Basic attackers from Operation Dream Job target transportation systems using phishing to gain initial access, execute malicious files, and impact operations through data destruction.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, File System, Network Infrastructure

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2023-36845 (Suggested): Juniper Junos OS on EX Series and SRX Series contains a PHP external variable modification vulnerability.
stage.description: An email impersonating a legitimate entity is sent to employees, tricking them into clicking a link or opening an attachment leading to credential harvesting or malware download.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2023-28229 (Suggested): Microsoft Windows CNG Key Isolation Service contains an unspecified vulnerability allowing privilege escalation.
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 635; Name: Alternative Execution Due to Deceptive Filenames
stage.description: A malicious file disguised as a legitimate document is executed via reg.exe utility, enabling unauthorized command execution on the system.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 675; Name: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: Once inside the system, the attacker deletes or corrupts critical files, causing operational disruption and data loss.

Scenario.summary: Operation Dream Job employs phishing to gain initial access to transportation systems, executes malicious files to escalate privileges, and impacts operations by destroying critical data.
